evals.prompt:
Read the existing Drupal module at /tmp/phase19-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/ to understand its current code. This module has Project and Task content entities with CRUD routes, entity forms, access control handlers, dynamic permissions, a settings form, dashboard controller, a ProjectStatusBlock block plugin, Views integration, Twig templates, cron/queue processing, AI agent tools, REST API endpoints (GET tasks, PATCH status, PATCH fields, POST create, GET summary), a Vue.js Kanban board with drag-and-drop via vue-draggable-plus, a Drupal.behaviors bridge with once() guard, and a CSRF-aware API wrapper.

Extend this module with enhanced interactions, a task detail panel, and visual polish. Implement the following:

1. **Extended PHP Serialization**: Extend the serializeTask() method in BOTH KanbanController and TaskApiController to include:
   a. `description` field from the task entity (text_long field, may be NULL)
   b. `pictureUrl` in the assignee array — the user's profile picture as an absolute URL (use Drupal's file URL generation service, not hardcoded paths). Return null if no picture.
   c. Cast entity `id` fields to integers for JSON type consistency

2. **Project Members in drupalSettings**: Add a getProjectMembers() method to KanbanController that collects unique assignee user IDs from the project's tasks, loads those users, and returns an array of {id, name, pictureUrl}. Add this members array to drupalSettings.groupAiPm.kanban so the Vue filter dropdown can populate without loading all site users. Ensure the board's cache metadata accounts for all loaded user entities.

3. **DELETE API Endpoint**: Add a deleteTask() method to TaskApiController that deletes a task entity:
   - Must verify entity-level delete access before deletion
   - Return 204 No Content on success, 403 JSON error if access denied
   - Add a route for DELETE requests at /api/kanban/task/{task}
   - The route should return JSON error responses (not HTML) for error status codes
   - Protect the route against CSRF attacks for JavaScript callers (the existing PATCH/POST routes already demonstrate the correct mechanism)
   - The task parameter should be automatically resolved to an entity object
   - The route should use the admin theme

4. **JavaScript API Extension**: Add a deleteKanban() function to the existing API wrapper (js/src/api/drupal.js) that sends a DELETE request with CSRF protection following the same pattern as the existing patchKanban/postKanban functions. DELETE has no request body and returns no JSON (204).

5. **Vue Composables** — Create 4 new composable files in js/src/composables/:
   - **useToast.js**: Toast notification queue management. Provides addToast(message, type, duration) and removeToast(id). Module-level singleton via ref(). Types: success (auto-dismiss 3s), error (5s), info (4s). Track timeout IDs for manual dismiss cleanup.
   - **useFilters.js**: Filter state with URL sync. Returns reactive filters (assignee, priority), clearFilter(key), clearAll(), hasActiveFilters computed. Reads URL query params on mount via URLSearchParams. Watches filter changes and syncs back to URL via history.replaceState(). Deletes params with null values.
   - **useContextMenu.js**: Context menu positioning. Returns visible, position, task, show(event, taskData), hide(). Calculates position from event.clientX/clientY. Adjusts for viewport edge overflow via nextTick + getBoundingClientRect. Closes on document click and Escape key.
   - **useDisplayOptions.js**: Display preferences persisted in localStorage. Accepts projectId parameter. Returns options ref (showPriority, showAssignee, showDueDate, compactMode) and toggleOption(key). Reads from localStorage key `gapm-display-${projectId}` on creation, merges with defaults. Watches and writes changes back.

6. **New Vue Components**:
   - **AssigneeAvatar.vue**: Props: name, pictureUrl, userId. Shows user picture or colored-initials fallback circle. Deterministic background color from userId % colors array.
   - **ToastContainer.vue**: Renders toast notification stack using Vue's Teleport to escape Drupal admin chrome. Uses TransitionGroup for enter/leave animations. Fixed position at top-right with z-index above Drupal's toolbar. Import useToast composable internally.
   - **TaskDetailPanel.vue**: Slide-over panel from right side. Uses Teleport to render at body level. Shows full task metadata with editable status/priority/assignee via select elements. Close on overlay click, Escape key. Focus trap for accessibility. Emits close, update-task, delete-task.
   - **ContextMenu.vue**: Right-click menu rendered via Teleport at fixed position. Shows: Change Status (submenu with all statuses except current), Change Priority (submenu), Edit, Delete. Closes on outside click and Escape. Adjusts position for viewport edges.
   - **FilterBar.vue**: Rendered above board columns. Assignee dropdown (from members prop) and priority dropdown. Active filters shown as dismissible pills. Uses useFilters composable for state management.
   - **DisplayOptions.vue**: Dropdown button with checkboxes for showPriority, showAssignee, showDueDate, compactMode. Uses useDisplayOptions composable.

7. **Enhanced TaskCard.vue**:
   - Double-click on title activates inline editing (input field, Enter saves, Escape cancels, blur saves)
   - Single click on card body emits open-panel event (200ms delay to distinguish from double-click)
   - Due date visual warnings: computed class returning gapm-task-card--overdue (past due), gapm-task-card--due-today, gapm-task-card--due-soon (within 3 days)
   - Import and display AssigneeAvatar component
   - Right-click emits context-menu event with {event, task}
   - Keep existing keyboard-accessible status dropdown menu (WCAG 2.5.7)
   - New emits: open-panel, update-task, context-menu

8. **Enhanced Drag Animations**: Update KanbanColumn's VueDraggable with animation duration, easing curve, chosen-class for elevated card during drag. Update CSS: ghost with reduced opacity and dashed border, chosen with elevated shadow and slight rotation, column highlight on drag-over.

9. **useKanban.js Enhancement**: Import useToast, call addToast() on all error rollbacks ('error' type) and successful operations ('success' type). Add deleteTask(taskId) method with optimistic removal and rollback on error. Import deleteKanban from API wrapper.

10. **KanbanBoard.vue Wiring**: Import all new components. Add filteredColumns computed that filters by assignee and priority from useFilters. Pass displayOptions down through KanbanColumn to TaskCard for conditional property rendering. Handle events: open-panel (set selectedTask), context-menu (show context menu), context menu actions (status/priority/edit/delete), panel update/delete. Include ToastContainer.

11. **KanbanColumn.vue Updates**: Accept and forward displayOptions prop to TaskCard. Forward new TaskCard events (open-panel, update-task, context-menu) to parent via emit. Add drag-start/drag-end events.

12. **CSS Updates** in kanban.css (global, not scoped — SortableJS-injected classes need unscoped CSS):
   - Enhanced drag states: ghost opacity 0.4 with dashed border, chosen with elevated shadow and rotation, column drag-over highlight
   - Due date warnings: overdue red left border, due-today amber, due-soon dotted amber
   - Toast and panel transition animations
   - Compact mode reduced padding

13. **Vite Rebuild**: After all changes, run the Vite build to produce updated js/dist/kanban.js and js/dist/style.css.

Create all new files and modify existing files in /tmp/phase19-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/
After creating/modifying files, run: cd /tmp/phase19-VARIANT && ddev drush cr 2>/dev/null; true
Then build the Vue app: cd /tmp/phase19-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/js && npm install && npx vite build 2>/dev/null; true
Do NOT ask questions -- just create the code.
evals.expected_output: Extended module with DELETE route using _csrf_request_header_token and _format:json, entity upcasting, deleteTask with entity access check, serializeTask with pictureUrl via file_url_generator, members in drupalSettings with user cache tags, deleteKanban with X-CSRF-Token, 4 composables, 6 new Vue components, enhanced TaskCard with inline editing and due date warnings, enhanced drag animations, KanbanBoard wiring with filtered columns.
expectations: The DELETE route for task deletion uses _csrf_request_header_token: 'TRUE' in its requirements, NOT _csrf_token: 'TRUE' (the module already has PATCH/POST routes using _csrf_request_header_token which validates a token sent via X-CSRF-Token request header — using _csrf_token would require the token in the URL query string which JavaScript fetch() does not naturally provide; the DELETE route must use the same header-based CSRF mechanism as the existing mutation routes)

The DELETE route includes _format: json in its requirements section (without _format:json, Drupal returns HTML error pages for 403/404 instead of JSON responses — the existing API routes all include _format:json and the new DELETE route must follow the same pattern for consistent JSON error responses to JavaScript callers)

The DELETE route has entity upcasting configuration via options.parameters.task.type: 'entity:task' (without explicit upcasting config for the custom task entity type, Drupal may not automatically resolve {task} to a loaded entity object, causing the controller to receive a raw ID string instead of a TaskInterface object — the existing PATCH routes demonstrate this same pattern)

The DELETE route includes options._admin_route: TRUE (routes under /admin/ path prefix do NOT automatically inherit admin theme — without _admin_route: TRUE, the route renders with the frontend theme instead of the admin theme; all existing API routes in this module set _admin_route: TRUE)

The deleteTask() controller method calls $task->access('delete') to verify entity-level delete access BEFORE calling $task->delete(), NOT relying solely on the route-level _permission requirement (route permission only checks 'can this user access the endpoint' — entity access handlers check 'can this user delete THIS specific entity', which is essential for own-vs-any permission patterns in the access control handler)

The extended serializeTask() method generates the user profile picture URL using Drupal's file_url_generator service (\Drupal::service('file_url_generator')->generateAbsoluteString() or via dependency injection) to create an absolute URL from the file entity's URI, NOT by hardcoding /sites/default/files/ or constructing the path manually (the file_url_generator service handles stream wrappers, CDN configurations, and base URL correctly regardless of deployment environment)

The KanbanController board() method adds a 'members' array to drupalSettings.groupAiPm.kanban containing user data (at minimum id and name) collected from the project's task assignees, so the Vue filter dropdown can populate without loading all site users (this is server-rendered initial state following the same pattern as the existing tasks data in drupalSettings — the controller loads entities, serializes them, and passes them via #attached.drupalSettings)

The KanbanController board() method's render array cache metadata includes user entity cache tags or dependencies for users loaded when serializing assignee pictures and building the members array (via addCacheableDependency, getCacheTags, or adding user entity tags to #cache.tags — without user-specific cache tags, changes to user profiles like display name or profile picture will not invalidate the board cache, showing stale assignee information until manual cache clear)

The deleteKanban() function in the JavaScript API wrapper fetches a CSRF token from /session/token and includes it as an X-CSRF-Token request header on the DELETE request, following the exact same pattern as the existing patchKanban() and postKanban() functions (Drupal's _csrf_request_header_token route requirement validates this specific header — the token fetch and header name must match the existing CSRF pattern in the API wrapper)

The VueDraggable component in KanbanColumn.vue includes an animation prop (e.g., :animation="150" or animation="150") to enable smooth drag transition animations (without animation config, SortableJS moves cards instantly with no visual transition, making drag-and-drop feel jarring and unpolished — this is a key SortableJS configuration that controls the settle animation duration in milliseconds)

The TaskDetailPanel component uses Vue's Teleport (or teleport) directive with target 'body' or equivalent to render the slide-over panel outside the Kanban board's component tree (without Teleport, the panel's fixed positioning and z-index would be constrained by the Drupal admin chrome's overflow and stacking context, potentially rendering behind toolbar elements or being clipped by parent containers)

The useFilters composable syncs filter state to the browser URL using history.replaceState() (or pushState) so that filter selections persist across page reloads and can be shared via URL (without URL sync, refreshing the page loses all filter selections, and users cannot bookmark or share a filtered board view — replaceState is preferred over pushState to avoid polluting browser history with filter changes)

The useDisplayOptions composable reads from and writes to localStorage using a project-specific key (containing the projectId parameter) so that display preferences (showPriority, showAssignee, showDueDate, compactMode) persist across browser sessions and are scoped per project (without localStorage persistence, users must re-configure display options every time they visit the board; without project-scoped keys, all boards share the same display settings)

The CSS includes a gapm-task-card--overdue class (or similar BEM class for overdue state) that applies a visible left border (red/error color) to visually distinguish overdue tasks from normal tasks (the due date warning system uses CSS classes computed from date comparison in TaskCard — the overdue class is the most critical visual indicator as it flags tasks that have passed their deadline)

The ToastContainer component positions itself with a z-index value of 1000 or higher to ensure toast notifications render above Drupal's admin toolbar (z-index ~500-600) and other admin chrome elements (without sufficient z-index, toasts render behind the toolbar and are invisible to the user, defeating the purpose of feedback notifications)

The serializeTask() method in both KanbanController and TaskApiController outputs the 'created' and 'changed' fields as ISO 8601 date strings (e.g., date('c', $timestamp)) rather than raw Unix timestamps from getCreatedTime()/getChangedTime() (JavaScript Date constructor interprets raw integers as milliseconds, not seconds — Unix timestamp 1741500000 becomes January 1970 instead of 2025; ISO 8601 strings parse correctly in all browsers and timezone-aware)
